text: I said I was going to wake up at 6am and write before work. That lasted exactly three days. Turns out I like the idea of being a morning person way more than actually being one. The only thing that consistently works is blocking 45 minutes after dinner and putting my phone in the other room. Not glamorous, but it’s real.
label: human

text: We shipped the feature on Friday and by Monday morning support had 17 tickets about it. None of them were about the thing we were worried about. Every single one was about the tiny copy change in the modal. I love and hate users equally.
label: human

text: I ran the numbers three times because I thought I messed up the spreadsheet. Nope. We’re spending more on SaaS per month than we were on rent in 2018. Half of it is tools we ‘might’ need. This is my annual reminder that convenience compounds.
label: human

text: My dad still prints out Google Maps directions before driving somewhere new. I asked him why and he said, 'Because paper doesn’t lose signal.' I didn’t have a comeback for that.
label: human

text: There’s a very specific kind of tired that comes from context switching 12 times in a day. It’s not physical. It’s like your brain tabs are all still open and playing music at the same time.
label: human

text: I used to think I hated networking. Turns out I just hate loud rooms where no one actually listens. Small dinners with six curious people? Completely different experience.
label: human

text: The first time I tried to negotiate salary I rehearsed the sentence for two hours and still opened with 'sorry, I just had a quick question.' Painful. I got better. But wow that muscle was weak.
label: human

text: I don’t trust anyone who says they 'love meetings.' I like decisions. I like clarity. Meetings are just the tax we pay to get there.
label: human

text: Tried the 75 hard thing for exactly 11 days. Day 12 I was arguing with myself about whether walking counted as a workout. That’s when I realized the plan was running me, not the other way around.
label: human

text: Our group chat has been silent for two weeks and I keep opening it like something is going to magically appear. Friendship maintenance is way less automatic in your 30s than anyone warned me about.
label: human

text: Every time I think I understand how taxes work I discover a new form that proves I do not. The IRS website feels like it was designed by someone who actively resents joy.
label: human

text: I said yes to a project I probably shouldn’t have because I liked the person asking. That’s the whole story. No strategic angle. Just vibes.
label: human

text: The best manager I ever had would wait three full seconds before responding to anything. At first I thought he hadn’t heard me. Eventually I realized he was just thinking. I’ve tried to copy that. It’s harder than it looks.
label: human

text: We keep calling it 'burnout' but for me it was more like boredom plus pressure plus too many Slack notifications. The fix wasn’t a vacation. It was deleting two channels.
label: human

text: I don’t actually want a morning routine. I want the feeling of having my life together. Those are not the same thing, which is inconvenient.
label: human
